claim: Quasar Alpha (OpenRouter stealth model) achieves near-Sonnet performance with 4x faster speed and 1M token context
actionable: For cost/speed-critical agent work, test Quasar Alpha; benchmark inference latency and token costs vs Claude Sonnet for your workload.
source_title: BRAND NEW Stealth LLM Released on OpenRouter

claim: Quasar Alpha shows strong performance on coding benchmarks, nearly matching o1-mini while maintaining speed advantage
actionable: When building coding agents, consider Quasar Alpha as alternative to larger reasoning models; measure actual throughput vs quality.
source_title: BRAND NEW Stealth LLM Released on OpenRouter

claim: Speed is critical for agent systems; faster inference models enable real-time multi-step workflows that reasoning models cannot support
actionable: For multi-step agent loops, prioritize fast inference (sub-second) over perfect reasoning; use smaller/faster models for agent orchestration.
source_title: BRAND NEW Stealth LLM Released on OpenRouter

claim: OpenRouter provides free access to pre-release models via API; useful for testing new capabilities before release
actionable: Monitor OpenRouter for stealth models; set up automated benchmarks to track new model releases and evaluate for production use.
source_title: BRAND NEW Stealth LLM Released on OpenRouter

claim: 1M token context length may not be fully working in early pre-release; test context limits before relying in production
actionable: When using pre-release models with advertised large context, empirically test context boundaries; expect potential 400 errors at limits.
source_title: BRAND NEW Stealth LLM Released on OpenRouter
